subject: [PATCH v3 09/11] mm: handle PMD swap entry faults on swap-in

2026-07-03.patch_summary: This patch adds a new handler for PMD swap entry faults on swap-in, allowing the kernel to handle large page mappings in one shot. It introduces a new function do_huge_pmd_swap_page() that resolves the entire 2 MB mapping by looking up the folio in the swap cache, allocating a PMD-order folio if necessary, and restoring soft_dirty and uffd_wp from the swap PMD. The handler also updates vmf->orig_pmd and calls wp_huge_pmd() to COW immediately when the resulting PMD is read-only but the fault was a write. If PMD-order resources are unavailable, it splits the PMD swap entry into 512 PTE swap entries via __split_huge_pmd().
